title: vendor/github.com/docker/docker/hack: Remove unused directory
body:
This directory just had Markdown and a `vendor.conf`.  I'm not sure why we have it in our version control, maybe old versions of vndr kept it?  Or maybe folk dropped it into `vendor/` by hand without using vndr?  The history of that vendored directory is:

* 619637a9 (Handle Linux Capabilities from command line, 2017-11-03, #17) added the three files to our version control.
* c344fe61 (Update vendoring, 2017-11-22, #60) bumped `hack/README.md`.
* af64e104 (Vendor in lots of kubernetes stuff to shrink image size, 2018-03-26, #554) bumped `hack/README.md`.
* 27107fda (Vendor in latest containers/image and contaners/storage, 2018-04-18, #509) removed the files.
* a824186a (Use buildah commit and bud in podman, 2018-04-25, #681) added the files back.
* I'm removing them again in this commit.

With this commit,

```console
$ vndr github.com/docker/docker
```

becomes a no-op.

Spun off from #747.
